name: MQ135 Gas Sensor
fullName: MQ135 Air Quality & Gas Detection Sensor
tagline: Detects CO₂, ammonia, benzene, smoke and harmful gases for air quality monitoring
description: The MQ135 is a semiconductor gas sensor that detects a wide range of harmful gases including CO₂, ammonia (NH₃), benzene, alcohol vapors, and smoke. It outputs both an analog voltage proportional to gas concentration and a digital HIGH/LOW signal when gas exceeds a threshold set by the onboard potentiometer. Widely used in air quality monitors and ventilation control systems.

specs.detectableGases: CO₂, NH₃, NOₓ, alcohol, benzene, smoke
specs.packageType: Breakout module with DO and AO pins

pinout.name: Analog Out
pinout.desc: Analog voltage proportional to gas concentration (connect to A0)

pinout.name: Digital Out
pinout.desc: HIGH when gas is below threshold, LOW when above (threshold set by pot)

wiringNote: The sensor requires a warm-up time of ~30 seconds per session (and ideally 24 hours of continuous use for accurate calibration). Never use it as a precise CO₂ ppm meter without calibration in clean air.
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'MQUnifiedsensor' → Install (optional for ppm calculations)
commonMistakes: Using readings immediately on power-up → inaccurate; sensor needs 30-second warm-up minimum
Expecting accurate ppm readings without calibration in clean air
Powering with 3.3V → heater doesn't reach operating temperature, false readings
Confusing AO and DO pins → AO gives analog data, DO gives threshold alert
